resolvedGaps.closure: OIDC and NHS login handling are isolated behind AuthTransaction and bridge callback contracts instead of route-local controller logic.
Post-auth return is a PostAuthReturnIntent bound to lineage, route family, subject, binding version, session epoch, channel manifest, and selected anchor. It is not a free-form URL.
Identity-provider success does not create a Vecells session until SessionEstablishmentDecision evaluates the callback, return intent, capability ceiling, and local session policy.
Logout, timeout, revocation, downgrade, and stale-return outcomes settle through SessionTerminationSettlement and session projection semantics.
Patient-facing sign-in, callback holding, consent-declined, re-auth, session-expired, subject-conflict, stale-return, and bounded-recovery states are frozen as safe same-shell grammar.
